title: JD Fit Decoder — User Profile Schema
description: Structured representation of the job seeker's background, used as the basis for scoring job descriptions against. Edit profile/my_profile.json to update.

seniority_level.description: Your self-assessed current seniority level, used to compare against seniority signals extracted from a JD.

total_years_experience.description: Total years of relevant professional experience (PM/BA/analyst roles).

skills.description: Specific, nameable skills — not job titles. Each skill is independently matchable against a JD requirement.

name.description: Skill name, kept consistent and specific (e.g. 'SQL', 'Stakeholder management', 'A/B testing', 'Roadmapping'). Avoid vague entries like 'communication'.

years.description: Years of hands-on experience with this specific skill (can be less than total_years_experience).

proficiency.description: Self-assessed depth. 'familiar' = used a few times, 'expert' = could teach/mentor others.

evidence.description: Optional one-line proof point (e.g. 'Led SQL-based cohort analysis at BYJU'S'). Used for the score breakdown rationale, not for matching logic.

domains.description: Industry/domain experience (e.g. 'EdTech', 'SaaS', 'B2B', 'B2C'). Used to score domain-fit requirements.

tools.description: Named tools/platforms (e.g. 'Jira', 'Figma', 'Amplitude', 'Looker'). Kept separate from skills because JDs often name tools explicitly and this is a cheap, high-precision match.

certifications.description: Named certifications (e.g. 'CSPO'). JDs sometimes name these explicitly as nice-to-haves or differentiators.

education.description: Optional. Only needed if a JD has hard education requirements (e.g. 'Master's degree required').

location.description: Used only for location/visa-requirement flags, not for skill scoring.

work_authorization.description: e.g. 'EU/EEA citizen', 'visa required', 'Stamp 4' — used to flag JDs with location/eligibility blockers.
